reasoning: While programmatic text scores are high (96%+ recall and precision), the LLM fidelity evaluation identifies a significant visual and structural failure. Specifically, the omission of a data-rich map and its associated legend on the first page represents a major loss of context that programmatic shingling cannot detect. The low visual score (0.5) and the 'needs_retry' pipeline status, combined with the cost of a false negative, require an escalation to human review.

concerns.analysis: In Section 3 (corresponding to slide 3), the heading text '2025 Data Collection Area' and 'North Carolina Collaboratory' is duplicated. These strings are concatenated to form the section title ('2025 Data Collection Area - North Carolina Collaboratory') and then appear again as individual heading elements within the section. concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the first page is significantly altered in the HTML conversion. In the source PDF, Page 1 is organized around a central map of North Carolina that provides spatial context for the text boxes and legend. In the HTML output, this map is entirely missing. This results in the associated text (regarding phases and quality levels) being presented as a disjointed list without its primary organizational anchor. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 of the PDF features a central map titled 'North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase' with informational text boxes overlaid and a legend positioned below.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML section 'NC Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) Elevation Data 2024 Data Collection' contains only the triangle logo and the airplane diagram followed by plain text, completely omitting the map and its surrounding layout structure.

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Most critically, the large map of North Carolina colored by phase ('North Carolina LiDAR Quality Level By Phase') and its corresponding legend ('Phase & Quality') on Page 1 are absent. Additionally, the DPS and EMAP logos that appear on every page of the source PDF are missing in the HTML, although the NC Emergency Management triangle logo is preserved. The omission of the data-rich map is a major defect.
